channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Microsoft 365 Quarterly Uptime Number Sinks to New Low
item.description: According to Microsoft, the Microsoft 365 uptime performance for Q1 CY2026 was 99.526%. That’s the lowest uptime figure since we started to record data in 2013. The first thing that people might ask is whether this performance means that Microsoft has to pay out under the terms of its financially...
item.dc:creator: TonyRedmond

item.title: If You're Building AI on Azure, ECS 2026 is Where You Need to Be
item.description: Let me be direct: there's a lot of noise in the conference calendar. Generic cloud events. Vendor showcases dressed up as technical content. Sessions that look great on paper but leave you with nothing you can actually ship on Monday. ECS 2026 isn't that. As someone who will be on stage at Cologne...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: NFS Permission Denied in Azure App Service on Linux: What It Means and What to Do
item.description: If your Azure App Service on Linux uses an Azure Files NFS share, you may sometimes see errors like Permission denied or Errno 13 when your app tries to write to the mounted path. Azure Files supports NFS for Linux and Unix workloads, and NFS uses Unix-style numeric ownership and permissions...
item.dc:creator: michelleyau

item.title: Getting Started with the SUSE Multi-Linux Manager MCP Server and GitHub Copilot
item.description: Enterprise Linux environments are heterogeneous. That's not a problem statement - it's just the truth. SUSE, Ubuntu, RHEL, and their downstream variants coexist in every data center I've seen, and increasingly across Azure subscriptions too. AI assistants like GitHub Copilot can already connect to...
item.dc:creator: abbottkarl

item.title: Continuous feedback in Teams without it getting lost in chat?
item.description: Our company is trying to move from annual reviews to continuous feedback. The idea is that managers and peers give quick feedback throughout the year and it all gets captured somewhere. Right now people just send feedback in Teams chat or email and it vanishes. By the time reviews come around...
item.dc:creator: ElisaDelaney

item.title: How to delete contacts on iPhone in bulk?
item.description: I am trying to find a way to delete multiple contacts at once on my iPhone because manually removing each contact one by one is very time-consuming and inconvenient. I want to know if there are any built-in features, settings, or reliable third-party apps that can help me quickly select and delete...
item.dc:creator: Brondam

item.title: Azure SDK Release (April 2026)
item.description: Azure SDK releases every month. In this post, you'll find this month's highlights and release notes. The post Azure SDK Release (April 2026) appeared first on Azure SDK Blog.
item.dc:creator: Ronnie Geraghty

item.title: Ingest at Scale, Securely — Azure Monitor pipeline Is Now Generally Available
item.description: Today, we're thrilled to announce the general availability of Azure Monitor pipeline — a telemetry pipeline built for secure, high-scale ingestion across any environment. But the best way to understand what makes it powerful isn't to start with features. It's to start with the problems that kept...
item.dc:creator: XemaPathak

item.title: Security Copilot for SOC: bringing agentic AI to every defender
item.description: Cybersecurity has entered an era of relentless complexity. As threat actors increasingly leverage artificial intelligence to automate attacks, evade detection, and scale their tactics, defenders are challenged to keep up. In this new era, security operations centers (SOCs) must transform to not...
item.dc:creator: cristinadagamah

item.title: The Polyglot tax – Part 4
item.description: The Agent-Ready Database: Security, Backup, and MCP Part 4 of 4 – The Multi-Model Database Series This is the final post in a four-part series on multi-model databases in SQL Server 2025 and Azure SQL – exploring how the optimizer, storage engine, and security layer treat each data model as a...
item.dc:creator: Aditya Badramraju

item.title: .NET 10.0.7 Out-of-Band Security Update
item.description: We are releasing .NET 10.0.7 as an out-of-band security update to address CVE-2026-40372. The post .NET 10.0.7 Out-of-Band Security Update appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Rahul Bhandari (MSFT)

item.title: Announcing TypeScript 7.0 Beta
item.description: Today we are absolutely thrilled to announce the release of TypeScript 7.0 Beta! If you haven’t been following TypeScript 7.0’s development, this release is significant in that it is built on a completely new foundation. Over the past year, we have been porting the existing TypeScript codebase from...
item.dc:creator: Daniel Rosenwasser

item.title: I Wasted 68 Minutes a Day Re-Explaining My Code. Then I Built auto-memory.
item.description: ~1,900 lines of Python. Zero dependencies. Saves you an hour a day. GitHub → · pip install auto-memory Now give Copilot CLI enhanced context recall. Point it at deploy/install.md and let it cook. 🍳 Are you tired of using the slash /compact command every 10 min? The Context Window Is a Lie Every AI...
item.dc:creator: Desi Villanueva

item.title: General Availability: Dynamic Data Masking for Azure Cosmos DB
item.description: Protecting sensitive data is a foundational requirement for modern applications. As organizations scale their use of Azure Cosmos DB across teams, environments, and workloads, controlling who can see sensitive information becomes increasingly important. Today, we are excited to announce the general...
item.dc:creator: Sudhanshu Khera

item.title: Aspire 13.2: Bun Support and Container Enhancements
item.description: Aspire 13.2 keeps the JavaScript story moving with Bun for Vite, more predictable containers, and small but meaningful fixes across debugging and builds. The post Aspire 13.2: Bun Support and Container Enhancements appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: David Pine

item.title: How to purge OneDrive from the default user folders
item.description: Hi. I got this PC a while ago and OneDrive just kinda set itself up based on my Microsoft account. It was decently useful having access to my OneDrive files as a folder on my PC but I do NOT want it backing up my files automatically, partly because I want those to be offline and because I really...
item.dc:creator: janthemanwlj

item.title: CodeQL now supports sanitizers and validators in models-as-data
item.description: CodeQL is the static analysis engine behind GitHub code scanning, which finds and remediates security issues in your code. You can now define custom sanitizers and validators using data extensions… The post CodeQL now supports sanitizers and validators in models-as-data appeared first on The GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Detection strategies across cloud and identities against infiltrating IT workers
item.description: The shift to remote and hybrid work since the pandemic expanded global hiring and accelerated digital onboarding, increasing reliance on online identity verification and remote access. The post Detection strategies across cloud and identities against infiltrating IT workers appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team and Microsoft Threat Intelligence

item.title: Announcing public preview of redundant TCP support for RDP Multipath for Azure Virtual Desktop
item.description: Reliable connectivity is essential for ensuring consistent productivity in Azure Virtual Desktop (AVD) environments. Network variability—whether due to packet loss, NAT misconfiguration, UDP‑restricted networks, or restrictive enterprise network policies—continues to be one of the most common...
item.dc:creator: Rinku_Dalwani

item.title: Intune application migration & app management
item.description: Migrating applications from Configuration Manager and other on-prem solutions to Microsoft Intune cloud native remains a challenging and time consuming undertaking, especially when dealing with complex line-of-business, legacy, and custom home-grown applications. Some organizations pursuing a full...
item.dc:creator: Lior_Bela

item.title: Innovations in OneDrive for collaboration, intelligence, and control
item.description: Last year, we made a promise: your files should work for you, not the other way around. That meant reimagining OneDrive not just as a place to store files, but as an intelligent layer that surfaces what matters, eliminates busy work, and enables seamless collaboration across your organization....
item.dc:creator: Arwa_Tyebkhan

item.title: Introducing Markdown support in SharePoint and OneDrive
item.description: As AI moves from answering questions to carrying out real work, teams need a reliable way to store, manage, and refine the instructions and context that guide how AI operates. Markdown files are increasingly becoming that foundation, acting as durable AI artifacts that capture rules, preferences,...
item.dc:creator: Arwa_Tyebkhan

item.title: Azure DevOps MCP Server April Update
item.description: This update brings a set of improvements and changes across both local and remote Azure DevOps MCP Servers. Here’s a summary of what’s changed. Query work items with WIQL We’ve introduced a new wit_query_by_wiql tool that enables users to construct and run work item WIQL queries. For our remote...
item.dc:creator: Dan Hellem

item.title: Agentic Fabric: How MCP is turning your data platform into an AI-native operating system
item.description: Something fundamental is changing in how developers interact with data platforms. Not a feature update, not a UI refresh, but a shift in the interface itself.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Getting Started with Agentic DevOps – Part 1: Foundations
item.description: This post is the first in a 3-part series: Foundations (this post) Start shipping (context engineering, MCP servers, orchestration) App modernization (Java, .NET, PHP, microservices) Bookmark this post for quick reference as you start exploring Agentic DevOps. It will be updated as the 3 parts...
item.dc:creator: Ricardo Fiel

item.title: Claim your IQ Series: Foundry IQ badge
item.description: The IQ Series kicked off with three Foundry IQ episodes, each paired with a hands-on cookbook. If you've worked through all three or you're planning to, there's now a digital badge waiting for you to claim! What the badge represents The IQ Series: Foundry IQ badge recognizes developers who've...
item.dc:creator: aycabas

item.title: My C drive is running out of space, so I want to upgrade to Windows 11 26H1 to clean up junk files.
item.description: My C drive is almost full, mainly because the WinSxS and AppData folders are taking up space and are difficult to delete. I’m thinking of upgrading to 26H1 to free up some space. Will CS:GO run without errors on 26H1? If it works fine, I plan to test it in a virtual machine first before installing...
item.dc:creator: Stonehu

item.title: Occasional frame drops when minimizing windows in Windows 11
item.description: My computer has sufficient performance, and the monitor has a high refresh rate. When I open a window, wait a while, and then minimize it, I experience frame drops, but this doesn’t happen if I interact with the window continuously. I’m looking for a solution.
item.dc:creator: Kodyon

item.title: My laptop gets stuck on the logo screen after restarting following a Windows 11 installation
item.description: After installing Windows 11, the system gets stuck on the brand logo screen during restart and won't boot into the OS. Please help with a solution.
item.dc:creator: Komondor2240

item.title: Teams Transcription (Licensing / Button greyed out for some users)
item.description: Hello,i have questions regarding transcription in Teams. Our org has Microsoft 365 Business Standard licenses, two users have icrosoft 365 Copilot Business on top of that.First question: is transcription possible for users without Copilot Business License?If the meeting policy is specified as...
item.dc:creator: AndiY

item.title: Windows 11 displays a black screen at startup and won't load the desktop
item.description: My computer suddenly shut down on its own. After restarting, the screen remains black, and I can't boot into the system normally. Please help.
item.dc:creator: Castiellm

item.title: After reinstalling Windows 11, the system frequently restarts while gaming
item.description: After reinstalling Windows 11, my computer frequently restarts on its own when I play games, but everything works fine during light use, such as watching videos or doing everyday office work. I’d like to know the possible causes, as well as how to troubleshoot and resolve the issue.
item.dc:creator: DaxxonSawyer

item.title: Right-clicking on taskbar shortcuts, Wi-Fi, or the clock area in Windows 11 also does not work
item.description: After the Windows 11 automatic update, I can left-click on taskbar shortcuts but cannot right-click them; when I click on the Wi-Fi, volume, or clock icons, I see a loading animation but they won’t open at all. The problem persists after restarting. Please help.
item.dc:creator: Wrentdr

item.title: How to free up space on iphone quickly when phone storage is full?
item.description: Hi everyone,I have an iPhone that keeps telling me my phone storage is full. I don't know much about phones, so I'm looking for simple steps to free up storage on my iPhone quickly.Here's what I want to learn:The fastest things to delete first – like maybe old texts, photos, or app data?How to...
item.dc:creator: garland

item.title: My iPhone screen frozen. How can I unfreeze an unresponsive iPhone?
item.description: I'm hoping someone can walk me through this.My iPhone screen is totally frozen—can't swipe, can't tap, and the phone doesn't react to anything. I can't turn off, too. The screen is just stuck on one app. What are the actual button sequences to force a restart on a modern iPhone?I've heard something...
item.dc:creator: OregonRain

item.title: The Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK and the additionalProperties Property
item.description: The additionalProperties property is available for many Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK cmdlets. In this article, we explain the function of the additionalProperties property and how it functions in holding output for Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK cmdlets. It’s all because of the lack of...
item.dc:creator: TonyRedmond

item.title: Need help for Windows 11 Pro ISO 64 bit download
item.description: I went to the Windows 11 ISO download page but there is no option for downloading Windows 11 Pro. In fact, I want to use BitLocker drive encryption on my PC. I understand that BitLocker is only available on the Pro, Enterprise, and Education editions of Windows 11, not on Home.Where is the best...
item.dc:creator: Hupixdel

item.title: Leveraging Azure Resource Graph Queries for Azure Redis Configuration
item.description: Scenario Many times, we receive requests for a quick and reliable way to review Azure Redis configurations such as SKU tiers, Redis versions, TLS settings, Microsoft Entra authentication status, and public network exposure. Traditionally, these checks are performed using PowerShell, Azure CLI, or...
item.dc:creator: Soma_Sekhara_Raju

item.title: Leveraging Azure Resource Graph Queries for Azure Storage Configuration
item.description: Scenario Many times, we receive requests for a quick and reliable way to check which Azure Storage features are enabled across subscriptions—such as SFTP, Hierarchical Namespace (HNS), or default access tiers. For such scenarios, customers can use PowerShell, Azure CLI, or REST APIs; however, these...
item.dc:creator: jainsourabh

item.title: How to Troubleshoot Azure Functions Host Startup Issue
item.description: Overview Azure Functions is a powerful serverless compute service that enables you to run event-driven code without managing infrastructure. When you deploy a Function App, the Azure Functions host is the runtime process responsible for discovering your functions, loading extensions and bindings,...
item.dc:creator: vikasgupta5

item.title: From Playwright Automation to Agent Driven Testing (GHCP in Action)
item.description: What Is Agent-Driven Testing? Agent-driven testing represents a revolutionary shift from traditional, hardcoded test automation to intelligent, adaptive testing powered by AI agents. Unlike conventional Playwright tests that rely on static selectors and predefined workflows, agent-driven testing...
item.dc:creator: syedarshad

item.title: Service Bus SBMP Retirement: What BizTalk Server 2020 Customers Need to Know
item.description: On September 30, 2026, the Azure Service Bus team will retire support for the Service Bus Messaging Protocol (SBMP). This is important BizTalk Server 2020 customers who use the BizTalk Service Bus (SB-Messaging) adapter, as SBMP is the protocol that adapter relies on today. To help customers...
item.dc:creator: hcamposu

item.title: GitHub Copilot meets Azure Developer CLI: AI-assisted project setup and error troubleshooting
item.description: The Azure Developer CLI (azd) now integrates with GitHub Copilot for AI-assisted project scaffolding and intelligent deployment error troubleshooting, helping you resolve common Azure errors like MissingSubscriptionRegistration, SkuNotAvailable, and StorageAccountAlreadyTaken without leaving the...
item.dc:creator: Kristen Womack

item.title: Writing Node.js addons with .NET Native AOT
item.description: The C# Dev Kit team replaced C++ Node.js addons with C# and Native AOT, removing a dependency on Python. This post walks through building a Node.js native addon entirely in C# using N-API, LibraryImport, and UnmanagedCallersOnly. The post Writing Node.js addons with .NET Native AOT appeared first...
item.dc:creator: Drew Noakes

item.title: Changes to GitHub Copilot Individual plans
item.description: We're making these changes to ensure a reliable and predictable experience for existing customers. The post Changes to GitHub Copilot Individual plans appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Joe Binder

item.title: Troubleshoot with OpenTelemetry in Azure Monitor - Public Preview
item.description: OpenTelemetry is fast becoming the industry standard for modern telemetry collection and ingestion pipelines. With Azure Monitor’s new OpenTelemetry Protocol (OTLP) support, you can ship logs, metrics, and traces from wherever you run workloads to analyze and act on your observability data in one...
item.dc:creator: KayodePrince

item.title: Autonomous AKS Incident Response with Azure SRE Agent: From Alert to Verified Recovery in Minutes
item.description: When a Sev1 alert fires on an AKS cluster, detection is rarely the hard part. The hard part is what comes next: proving what broke, why it broke, and fixing it without widening the blast radius, all under time pressure, often at 2 a.m. Azure SRE Agent is designed to close that gap. It connects...
item.dc:creator: hailukassa

item.title: Secure, Keyless Application Access with Managed Identities - Now GA in Azure Files SMB
item.description: As enterprises modernize applications and strengthen their security posture, identity is central to how applications access shared storage. Traditional identity models relying on account keys, stored credentials, or domain‑joined infrastructure add operational overhead and introduce security risks...
item.dc:creator: Priyanka-Gangal

item.title: Changes to GitHub Copilot plans for individuals
item.description: As shared in our recent blog post, we’re making the following changes to Copilot plans for individuals as part of our ongoing efforts to ensure service reliability and a sustainable… The post Changes to GitHub Copilot plans for individuals appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Microsoft 365 Security Best Practices for Enterprises
item.description: In today’s digital-first workplace, Microsoft 365 has become the backbone of enterprise productivity. From email and file storage to collaboration tools like Teams and SharePoint, it centralizes critical business operations. But with this convenience comes a growing attack surface. Cyber threats...
item.dc:creator: JohnNaguib
